name: Strata
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Kern County, California for 75 years, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and investment services to local communities.
description_long:
Strata Credit Union (formerly Kern Federal Credit Union) has been operating in Kern County, California for 75 years, establishing itself as a community-focused financial institution. The credit union is NCUA-insured and maintains a strong commitment to local reinvestment and member relationships. They serve individuals and organizations across the region with a network of branches and ATM access.

Strata offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, youth accounts with Greenlight Cards, multiple loan types (auto, home, personal, credit builder, motorcycle/boat/RV, student loan refinance), mortgages including ITIN mortgages, home equity products, credit cards, and investment/insurance services. They provide digital banking through online and mobile platforms, bill payment services (Strata Pay), financial wellness tools, calculators, and investment advisory services. Competitive rates are advertised: credit cards at 10.75% APR, auto loans at 5.49% APR, and mortgages at 5.000% APR.

Strata distinguishes itself through demonstrated community involvement, having sponsored athletic programs and school activities at Wasco High School and other local organizations. Member testimonials emphasize personalized service, staff friendliness, and individualized attention that differentiates the credit union from larger financial institutions. The organization actively recruits membership and promotes benefits specific to credit union membership models, such as member-owned governance and community reinvestment practices.

As a credit union, Strata is subject to NCUA regulation and operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned basis. However, specific information about membership eligibility requirements, field of membership restrictions, account minimums, and detailed fee schedules is not comprehensively disclosed on the reviewed website pages. The profile suggests strong community ties but lacks transparent pricing and eligibility details typical of full financial service provider documentation.
services: Home equity loans and lines of credit
Personal loans and lines of credit
pros: 75-year history serving Kern County with established community trust and local presence
Multiple loan products including specialized offerings like ITIN mortgages, credit builder loans, and 0% computer loans
Comprehensive digital banking including mobile app, online banking, and Strata Pay bill payment
Member testimonials highlight exceptional personalized service and staff friendliness compared to large banks
Active community investment documented through school sponsorships and charitable giving programs
Competitive advertised rates: 5.49% APR auto loans, 5.000% APR mortgages, 10.75% APR credit cards
Youth financial education through Greenlight Card and youth account programs
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Kern County, California—not suitable for members relocating elsewhere
Website does not clearly disclose membership eligibility requirements or field of membership restrictions
Lack of transparent fee schedules, account minimums, or detailed product terms on publicly available website pages
No information provided about mobile app features, ATM network size/access, or branch hours
Investment services and insurance offerings are mentioned but not detailed on accessible website content
best_for: Kern County residents seeking personalized banking with emphasis on community relationships and local reinvestment
Small business owners and organizations requiring business lending and treasury account services
Families prioritizing youth financial education through dedicated youth products and accounts
Borrowers needing specialized loan products like ITIN mortgages or credit builder loans with lower rates
diagnosis: Strata Credit Union is best for Kern County residents who value personalized, community-focused banking and are willing to work with a regional institution rather than a national bank. Primary caveat: membership eligibility and field of membership restrictions are not transparently disclosed online, requiring direct inquiry to confirm eligibility before account opening.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Strata — Credit Union in BAKERSFIELD, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Strata — NCUA-insured credit union in BAKERSFIELD, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
